info.title: Jiezi Cloud API
info.description: Jiezi Cloud document management system REST API

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of all users.
Requires `Admin` or `Owner`.

Returns the full user record for the given ID.
Requires `Admin` or `Owner`.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a user account and revoke all their sessions.
Requires `Owner`.  Cannot delete the last owner account.

patch.description:
Set or clear a user's storage quota.
Requires `Owner` (only the owner controls quotas).

post.description:
Force-reset a user's password without knowing their current one.
Requires `Admin` or `Owner`.

patch.description:
Change the system-level role of a user.

Authorization rules (enforced by the service layer):
- Owner may grant any role.
- Admin may only promote/demote between `Member` and `Guest`.
- Nobody may change their own role.

Suspend (`is_active: false`) or reactivate (`is_active: true`) a user.
Admin cannot suspend an Owner.

post.description:
Always returns `204 No Content` (even if the email is not registered)
to prevent email enumeration.

204.description: Password reset OTP sent (always 204 to prevent email enumeration)

post.description: Returns `200 OK` with a [`TokenPair`] on success.

post.summary: `POST /auth/logout` — revoke a refresh token (log out a device).

get.summary: `GET /auth/me` — return the current user's full profile.

patch.summary: `PATCH /auth/me` — update the current user's display name and / or avatar.
patch.description:
Only the fields present in the request body are updated.  Omit a field
entirely to leave it unchanged; set it to `null` to clear it.

post.summary: `POST /auth/me/password` — change the current user's own password.
post.description:
Requires the caller to supply their **current** password in `old_password`.
For admin-initiated forced resets see `POST /admin/users/{id}/reset-password`.

post.summary: `POST /auth/me/send-change-password-otp` — send OTP to own email before changing password.
post.description: No-op (204) when email verification is disabled in config.

204.description: OTP sent (or no-op if email verification is disabled)

post.summary: `POST /auth/refresh` — exchange a refresh token for a new pair (rotation).

post.description: Returns `201 Created` with the [`User`] object on success.

post.summary: `POST /auth/reset-password` — set a new password using the OTP.

post.description:
The client should call this before `POST /auth/register` when
`email.verification_required = true`.  Returns `204 No Content`.

post.summary: `POST /auth/send-unlock-otp` — email an unlock OTP for a locked account.

204.description: Unlock OTP sent (always 204 to prevent enumeration)

get.summary: `GET /auth/sessions` — list all active sessions for the current user.

post.summary: `POST /auth/unlock-account` — clear the lockout using the OTP.

get.description:
Supports the `Range` header for partial content delivery (HTTP 206).
Full files are returned with `200 OK`.

### Routing before I/O

The total file size is resolved from the VFS metadata first.  If the
resolved size exceeds the applicable per-client/per-mode limit, a
structured error is returned before any bytes are read from storage:
- Native client, size >= `large_file_threshold` → `426` (use QUIC).
- Web client, size > web limit → `413` (install native client).

413.description: File too large for web download

426.description: Use QUIC for large files (native client)

get.summary: `GET /files/trash` — list all soft-deleted nodes owned by the caller.

post.summary: `POST /files/{id}/copy` — copy a node (and subtree) under a new parent.
post.description: Returns the root of the copied subtree with a new ID.

post.summary: `POST /files/{id}/move` — move a node to a different parent.

delete.description:
Irreversible.  The node and all its descendants are removed from the
database.  Content deduplication means storage chunks are only freed
by a separate garbage-collection pass.

post.description:
Performs the first-run setup:
1. Validates the request body.
2. Creates the Owner account via [`AuthService::bootstrap_owner`].
3. Persists site settings to the `system_settings` table.
4. Marks setup as complete (DB + in-memory atomic flag).

Returns `409 Conflict` if setup has already been completed.

get.description:
Returns whether the server still needs to be configured.
Safe to call anonymously; returns no sensitive information.

post.description:
Accepts the raw file bytes in the request body.  The pipeline:
1. Receives raw bytes (Actix request body limit applies).
2. Routes based on client type and file size:
   - Native client, size >= `large_file_threshold` → `426 Upgrade Required` (use QUIC).
   - Web client, size > web limit → `413 Payload Too Large` (install native client).
3. Runs FastCDC chunking.
4. Writes chunks to all configured storage backends (dedup-aware).
5. Creates a `FileNode` record in the VFS under `parent_id`.

### Client identification

Set the `X-Jiezi-Client: native` request header to identify as the native
desktop / mobile client.  Absence of this header (or any other value) is
treated as a web-browser client.

Returns the new [`FileNode`] as JSON with `201 Created`.

413.description: File too large for web upload

426.description: Use QUIC for large files (native client)

new_password.description: The new password to assign.  Minimum 8 characters.

email_otp.description:
6-digit OTP sent to the account email.  Required when
`email_verification_required` is enabled in config.

old_password.description: The caller's current password (verified before accepting the change).

ChangeRoleRequest.description: Payload for `PATCH /admin/users/{id}/role` — change a user's system role.

new_parent_id.description: ID of the destination parent directory.

FileId.description: Identifies a file or directory node in the virtual file system.

FileMetadata.description: Extended, optional metadata attached to a file node.

extra.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs sourced from upload headers or EXIF data.

has_thumbnail.description: Whether a thumbnail has been generated for this file.

is_indexed.description: Whether the file content has been submitted to the search index.

tags.description: User-defined tags for filtering and organisation.

FileNode.description:
A node in the virtual file system — either a file or a directory.

File content is stored separately by the storage backend; this struct
holds only the metadata that lives in the database.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the full file content.

deleted_at.description: Set when the node has been soft-deleted (moved to trash).

mime_type.description: MIME type detected at upload time (e.g. `"image/jpeg"`).

oneOf.description: `None` only for the root node of a space.

size.description: File size in bytes.  Zero for directories.

LoginRequest.description: Payload for the user login endpoint.

credential.description: Either a username or an email address.

device_label.description:
Optional human-readable label for the device initiating the login.
Shown in the active sessions list so users can identify and revoke
specific devices (e.g. "iPhone 15", "Home PC — Firefox").

NodeType.description: The kind of a virtual file system node.

PageRequest.description: Pagination parameters sent by the client in query strings or request bodies.

per_page.description: Maximum number of items per page.

RegisterRequest.description: Payload for the user registration endpoint.

display_name.description: Optional friendly name shown in the UI.

ResetPasswordWithOtpRequest.description: Payload for `POST /auth/reset-password` — reset password using an OTP code.

code.description: The 6-digit OTP that was emailed via `POST /auth/forgot-password`.

Role.description:
User role — determines the base set of permissions.
Resource-level overrides can refine these defaults.

SendOtpRequest.description: Payload for `POST /auth/send-register-otp` — request an OTP for registration.

email.description: The email address to send the OTP to.

SessionInfo.description:
Represents a single active login session visible to the user.

Identified by its `family` UUID, which stays constant across all
refresh-token rotations within the same login session.

device_label.description: Human-readable label supplied by the client at login time.

expires_at.description: When the active refresh token in this session expires.

family.description: Stable identifier for this session (JWT rotation family UUID).

session_started.description: When the session was first created (i.e., when the user logged in).

storage_quota.description: Storage maximum in bytes.  `null` means unlimited.

SetupCompleteRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /setup/complete`.

All fields are validated server-side.  Passwords are never stored in plain
text; they are hashed with Argon2id before persistence.

admin_display_name.description: Optional display name shown in the UI (e.g. "System Administrator").

admin_email.description: Email address for the super-admin account.

admin_password.description: Password for the super-admin account (minimum 8 characters).

admin_username.description: Login username for the super-admin account (3-50 chars, alphanumeric/-/_).

registration_enabled.description:
Allow unauthenticated visitors to register new accounts.
Set to `false` for invite-only or enterprise deployments.

site_description.description: Optional short description / tagline for the site.

site_name.description: Human-readable site name shown in the web UI and emails.

setup_required.description: When `true` the server requires setup before it can be used.

SpaceId.description: Identifies a collaborative space (personal or shared).

TokenPair.description:
A short-lived access token paired with a longer-lived refresh token.
Returned after a successful login or token refresh.

UnlockWithOtpRequest.description: Payload for `POST /auth/unlock-account` — unlock a locked account using OTP.

code.description: The 6-digit OTP that was emailed via `POST /auth/send-unlock-otp`.

UpdateProfileRequest.description:
Payload for updating one's own profile (display name, avatar).

Each field is doubly-wrapped in `Option` so the client can distinguish
"omit this field" (`None`) from "clear this field" (`Some(None)`).

mime_type.description: Optional MIME type hint from the client.

parent_id.description: ID of the parent directory in the VFS.

avatar_url.description: Optional URL to the user's avatar image.

display_name.description: Optional display name shown in the UI.

email_verified.description:
Whether the user has verified their email address via OTP.

When `email.verification_required = true` in config, users must supply
the correct OTP code during registration before the account is confirmed.

is_active.description: Whether the account is active (not suspended).

storage_quota.description:
Maximum storage this user may consume across all their spaces,
in bytes.  `None` means unlimited.

bearer_auth.description: JWT access token — obtain from POST /api/v1/auth/login

tags.name: files
tags.description: Virtual file system — nodes, upload, download
